data.title: GfK: UK Consumer Confidence Improves in February
data.description:
Britain's consumer confidence indicator came in at -20 points in February, up from -22 points in January, market research company Growth from Knowledge said Friday.

Analysts expected -22 points for the month.

NIQ GfK consumer insights director Neil Bellamy said the biggest contributor to the increase was how consumers see their personal finances in the year ahead following the Bank of England's rate cut.

data.title: Shanghai Sheng Jian Environment Technology Plans Up to 500 Million Yuan Bond Issue
data.description:
Shanghai Sheng Jian Environment Technology is planning to issue up to 500 million yuan in convertible bonds to fund the expansion of its semiconductor auxiliary equipment and key components business.

Specifically, proceeds of 350 million yuan will be used for semiconductor equipment development and 150 million yuan for working capital, according to a filing with the Shanghai bourse.

The company's shares closed 3% higher Friday.

data.title: Airbus Consensus Estimates Expected to Be Trimmed — Market Talk
data.description: Investor sentiment regarding Airbus in the coming quarters should revolve around the European plane maker's ramp-up challenges and the integration of the aerostructures business, Jefferies analyst Chloe Lemarie says in a research note. Jefferies cuts its rating on the stock to hold from buy and trims its price target to 180 euros from 190 euros, saying it is waiting for greater tailwinds from the company's production ramp-up. A lack of share buyback implies less support for shares, Lemarie says. Jefferies expects consensus estimates will be trimmed over the next month and that shares will be put under pressure due to its plane deliveries. (pierre.bertrand@wsj.com)

data.title: Yuexiu Property's Subsidiary Wins Bid for Residential Use Land in Shanghai
data.description:
Yuexiu Property's subsidiary Hangzhou Yile Industrial Investment through a union with Shanghai Hongrun Real Estate won a bid for a land parcel in Yangsi, Pudong, Shanghai for 5.09 billion yuan, a Thursday bourse filing said.

The bid was won by way of an open tender for the land parcel intended for residential use. The land parcel has a total site area of 27,258 square meters and a total planned gross floor area of 104,369 square meters.

The company owns an effective attributable interest of 51.00% in the land parcel, and the parties to the union will establish a project company for the development of the land.

data.title: Saint-Gobain Appoints Maud Thuaudet as New CFO
data.description:
By Nina Kienle

Compagnie De Saint-Gobain said it appointed Maud Thuaudet as new chief financial officer, effective April 1.

The French building materials company on Friday said Thuaudet will succeed Sreedhar N., who held the position for more than six years.

The company appointed Sreedhar N. chief executive officer for the Asia-Pacific region, including Australia and New Zealand and India, effective also April 1, it said.

Write to Nina Kienle at nina.kienle@wsj.com

data.title: Scanwolf Advised to Accept Takeover Offer
data.description:
Scanwolf's independent adviser, Malacca Securities, has advised the company's shareholders to accept the mandatory takeover offer, deeming it fair and reasonable.

The privatization offer was triggered after Mighty Alliance acquired a 34.38% stake or 69.8 million shares in Scanwolf, according to a Thursday prospectus on Bursa Malaysia.

The manufacturer of plastic extrusions received a privatization offer by offerors to acquire the remaining shares at 0.54 ringgit apiece.

data.title: AD Ports' Noatum Maritime, ASRY Form Marine Services JV in Bahrain
data.description:
Noatum Maritime, a subsidiary of Abu Dhabi Ports (ADX:ADPORTS), d/b/a AD Ports, and Arab Shipbuilding & Repair Yard, or ASRY, formed a joint venture to offer marine services in Bahrain.

The joint venture aims to improve the services offered at the ASRY shipyard using Noatum Maritime's fleet and expertise, targeting external clients in the country, according to a Friday release.

The initial operational scope of the company will focus on towage and mooring using Noatum Maritime's tugboats and one of its mooring boats, with a plan to scale up to offer a full suite of maritime services.

data.title: U.K. Retail Sales Rise for First Time in Five Months — Update
data.description:
By Ed Frankl

Retailers in the U.K. reported a brighter start to the new year, with sales rising after four straight months of falls, though rebounding inflation and low consumer confidence suggest the outlook for the sector remains uncertain.

U.K. retail sales volumes were 1.7% higher in January than in the prior month, from a fall of 0.6% in December, the Office for National Statistics said Friday. Economists expected sales to climb 0.9% on month, according to a poll by The Wall Street Journal.

Food store sales volumes jumped 5.6%, the largest rise since March 2020, following falls in recent months, the ONS said.

However, retailers indicated that the increase was because of more people eating at home in January. That suggests retailers' gains come at a loss for restauranteurs and the boost to the overall economy will be smaller than these figures suggest, according to Capital Economics economist Paul Dales.

While consumer confidence in the U.K. edged up this month in data also published Friday, likely lifted by the Bank of England's interest-rate cut earlier in February, it remains at a low level. Indeed, sales fell over the three months to January and remain below prepandemic levels, the ONS said.

Elsewhere, inflation jumped to 3.0% in January, from 2.5% in the prior month, well above the central bank's target.

"The recent flatlining in employment and rebound in inflation may be offsetting the effects of the cuts in interest rates and holding back households' willingness to spend," Dales added.

Write to Ed Frankl at edward.frankl@wsj.com

data.title: Bilibili's Profitability Likely to Improve Further — Market Talk
data.description: Bilibili's profitability is likely to improve further, according to Daiwa analyst John Choi in a research note. "With 1Q25 gross profit margin likely to reach a similar level to 4Q24, we are optimistic about its gross margin expansion trajectory, with a long-term goal to surpass 40% level," Choi says. This is likely to be driven by high-margin core businesses, such as advertising and games, along with the flywheel effect of commercialisation, a robust content ecosystem and improving efficiency powered by AI tools, the analyst says. The analyst maintains a buy rating on the stock and raises its target price to HK$230.00 from HK$225.00. Shares are 15.5% higher at HK$178.80. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

data.title: Airbus Appears Conservative on Cash Flow Given Plane Delivery Target — Market Talk
data.description: Airbus's year-end results came in line with expectations except for its cash flow, which exceeded forecasts, Deutsche Bank analyst Christophe Menard says in a research note. The European plane maker's cash flow of 4.5 billion euros beat consensus of 3.65 billion euros, the analyst says. The company's guidance to deliver around 820 planes this year, as well as its target of around 7 billion euros in adjusted EBIT are broadly in line with expectations. But cash flow guidance appears conservative, the analyst says. Airbus guides for cash flow at 2024 levels this year, despite anticipating an extra 54 aircraft deliveries. (pierre.bertrand@wsj.com)

data.title: Sembcorp Industries Target Price Raised to S$7.38 From S$7.35 by DBS Group Research >U96.SG
Huazhong In-Vehicle to Boost Stake in Auto Parts Company
data.description:
A unit of Huazhong In-Vehicle agreed to purchase a further 15% equity interest in Ningbo Roekona-Zoeppritex-Tex-Line from Roekona Textilwerk for 4.5 million euros, a Thursday filing with the Hong Kong bourse said.

The target firm is currently 65% owned by buyer Ningbo Huazhong Automotive Decorative Parts Technology, 25% by Roekona Textilwerk and 10% by ZwissTex Germany. Following completion, the purchaser will hold an 80% stake in the target.

Ningbo Roekona-Zoeppritex-Tex-Line engages mainly in the manufacture and sale of auto parts, as well as the design and manufacturing of high-trade textiles.

data.title: Nordic Stocks Tipped to Open Slightly Higher — Market Talk
data.description: Nordic markets are seen opening slightly higher, with IG calling the OMXS30 up 0.2% at around 2731. U.S. stock markets fell yesterday while the U.S. dollar weakened broadly against most currencies and the U.S. 10-year yield is trading a few basis points lower, SEB analysts say in a note. The Hong Kong stock market is rallying this morning after a strong report from tech company Alibaba. The outcome from last night's talks between Ukraine and the U.S. is unclear, after a planned joint press conference following the talks was canceled. Germany's general election is Sunday and SEB says protracted government formation talks risk paralyzing Europe at a time when strong leadership is needed on trade negotiations with the U.S. and the situation in Ukraine. OMXS30 closed at 2725.09, OMXN40 at 2637.07 and OBX at 1414.98. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

0744 GMT - Nordic markets are seen opening slightly higher, with IG calling the OMXS30 up 0.2% at around 2731. U.S. stock markets fell yesterday while the U.S. dollar weakened broadly against most currencies and the U.S. 10-year yield is trading a few basis points lower, SEB analysts say in a note. The Hong Kong stock market is rallying this morning after a strong report from tech company Alibaba. The outcome from last night's talks between Ukraine and the U.S. is unclear, after a planned joint press conference following the talks was canceled. Germany's general election is Sunday and SEB says protracted government formation talks risk paralyzing Europe at a time when strong leadership is needed on trade negotiations with the U.S. and the situation in Ukraine. OMXS30 closed at 2725.09, OMXN40 at 2637.07 and OBX at 1414.98. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

0729 GMT - Prime US REIT's leasing activity is likely to improve in 2025, RHB Research's Vijay Natarajan says in a research report. The REIT expects to sign around 105,000 square feet of lease at Park Tower in California by 1H, the analyst notes. Enhancement of its Waterfront at Washingtonian asset in Maryland was completed in October with leasing rising to 49% from 40% in 3Q, and more leasing is anticipated in 1H, the analyst adds. The REIT's gearing and debt refinancing worries have mostly eased, and 2025 will probably be a year of stabilization before a full recovery in 2026. RHB maintains the REIT's buy rating and target price of US$0.23 on the units which are 0.6% higher at US$0.16. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0718 GMT - Chinese shares closed higher amid positive sentiment over tech stocks and Chinese AI firm DeepSeek. Alibaba's latest earnings are buoying the upbeat mood toward Chinese tech stocks and foreign investors' interest in Chinese equities after DeepSeek's tech breakthrough. GF Securities led the gains, up 2.1%. Citic Securities and Shenzhen Inovance Technology rose 2.0% each. CATL closed 2.0% higher after signing an MOU with Volkswagen on EV battery R&D. Meanwhile, Haier Smart Home closed 0.8% lower and ENN Natural gas fell 0.8%. The benchmark Shanghai Composite Index ended 0.85% higher at 3379.11 and the Shenzhen Composite Index ended 1.6% higher. The ChiNext Price Index added 2.5%. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

0654 GMT - NetEase may need a major blockbuster title launch for its stock to outperform in 2025, Daiwa analysts John Choi and Candis Chan say in a research note. the game maker's 4Q results didn't hold any major surprises, with revenue weighed by non-game businesses as management shifted its focus to improving profitability for these businesses, they say. The company also plans to adjust its team structures based on anticipated market demand to ensure operational efficiency, according to management. Daiwa thinks a key downside risk for NetEase would be lower-than-expected new games sales. Daiwa maintains a buy call on NetEase's H-shares but lowers its target to HK$180.00 from HK$195.00. Shares are last at HK$158.00. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

0650 GMT - Dohome's same-store sales growth may stay positive in 2025 due to low-base effects, but headwinds loom, CGS International's Chaiyatorn Sricharoen says in a research report. The brokerage expects only low-single-digit same-store sales growth for the home-improvement retailer, owing to headwinds including weak demand for building materials. Also, the Thai company will resume large-store expansion in 2Q, which could increase expenses, the analyst adds. The brokerage upgrades Dohome to hold from reduce after a sharp drop in the company's share price over the past month, which likely reflects weaker-than-anticipated recovery in building material demand. It maintains a target price of THB6.80 on the stock. Shares are 5.7% higher at THB7.40. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0647 GMT - Japanese stocks ended higher as concerns about borrowing costs ebb for now. Auto and pharmaceutical stocks led the gains. Chugai Pharmaceutical gained 4.5% and Subaru climbed 3.1%. The 10-year Japanese government bond yield dropped 2 basis points to 1.420% after Bank of Japan Gov. Kazuo Ueda promised to respond to abnormal rises in bond yields. The Nikkei Stock Average rose 0.3% to 38776.94. Investors are focusing on any developments in U.S. trade and foreign policies. USD/JPY is at 150.43, compared with 149.62 as of Thursday 5 p.m. Eastern time. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com; @kosakunarioka)

0632 GMT - The long-term outlook for Chinese stocks has turned more positive, Daiwa analysts write in a note. China's tech breakthroughs and pro-business policy pivot are game-changers for Chinese stocks, coming as concerns over a lack of long-term growth drivers and private-sector crackdowns ease, they say. In terms of fundamentals, China's prolonged property downturn will likely show structural improvements in 2H, Daiwa adds. Trump's reciprocal tariffs on other trading partners could take some of the heat off of China, while the discord between the U.S. and its European allies may present an opportunity for Beijing to repair its relations with the EU and explore new trade opportunities, they say. Daiwa raises its year-end target for the benchmark Hang Seng Index to 22000 from 20300. HSI last at 23314.40 (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

0619 GMT - Indian non-banking finance companies' credit costs seem to have peaked, setting the stage for improved profitability in the coming quarters, Nomura analysts say, noting the companies' 3Q FY 2025 earnings performance. With valuations having moderated, risk-reward dynamic for the sector appears more favorable now, the analysts say. A combination of stable asset quality, easing margin pressures, and better liquidity conditions could drive a re-rating in select names going forward, the analysts add. With easing liquidity conditions and more accommodative requlatory stance in India, these companies' loan disbursement growth trends should also improve in coming quarters. Nomura's top sector picks are Shriram Finance, Bajaj Finance, and Aadhar Housing Finance. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

0612 GMT - Samsung Electro-Mechanics is set to benefit this year from demand for its high-end chip package substrates for AI devices, Heungkuk Securities analyst Heechul Park writes in a note. Demand for the company's high-performance flip-chip ball grid array substrates should rise as the supply of cost-efficient AI servers increases, Park says. Its FC-BGA substrates for AI accelerators will start generating revenue from 2025, he adds. He expects operating profit at the company to jump 27% in 2025, following 14% growth in 2024. Heungkuk raises its target price for the stock by 12% to KRW190,000 and keeps a buy rating. Shares are 0.5% lower at KRW143,200. (kwanwoo.jun@wsj.com)

0609 GMT - Chinese equities may face a short-term pullback although the outlook remains positive in the long term, Daiwa analysts write in a note. They suggest investors take a breather after the recent rally, which was fueled by the rise of DeepSeek and China's recent symposium with business leaders to support the private sector. Sentiment-wise, Daiwa warns that stimulus measures announced at China's Two Sessions meeting in March may disappoint investors due to elevated expectations. Additionally, the completion of U.S. reviews on reciprocal tariffs and the U.S.-China phase-one trade deal by April 1 could reignite concerns over a potential trade war, they add.(jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

0548 GMT - China's electric-vehicle sector could see consolidation accelerating as more autonomous driving features are adopted in mass market cars, HSBC Global Research analysts write in a note. BYD's aggressive autonomous driving push makes higher level autonomy functions more accessible for its mass market models with price tags below CNY100,000, they add. Other Chinese automakers are likely to follow BYD's autonomous driving adoption, they say. The availability of higher-level autonomy in mass market and budget models could raise the bar in EV competition, and boost consolidation in the industry, they add. The companies with stronger AD features and higher in-house software capabilities are likely to better expand its market share, HSBC says.(jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

0546 GMT - Alibaba's cloud business revenue is expected to accelerate due to a strong uptick in AI demand, according to HSBC Global Research analysts. They note that the core business revenue for the December quarter was a positive surprise, driven by better-than-expected customer management, commission growth and cloud expansion. As a result, the brokerage lifts its fiscal year 2026 cloud revenue growth forecast to 17%, up from the previous 12%. Analysts also highlight the potential for upside catalysts in Alibaba's AI cloud revenue, with a surge in demand observed from January to February. Approximately 60% to 70% of new customer demand is related to AI inference, they add. HSBC maintains a buy rating for Alibaba's ADR and raises the target price to $160.00 from $128.00. The ADRs last closed at $135.97. (jiahui.huang@wsj.com; @ivy_jiahuihuang)

data.title: UK Market Talk Roundup: Brokers Comments
data.description:
Broker comments in the UK today. Compiled by Dow Jones Newswires Markets Desk, markets.eu@dowjones.com

European stocks are expected to open slightly higher, with Germany's DAX up 0.1% and France's CAC 40 up 0.3%, according to IG. Chinese stocks rose after Alibaba earnings buoyed sentiment toward Chinese tech stocks. The mood was less upbeat on U.S. stock markets, however, after weak guidance from retailer Walmart. The U.K.'s FTSE 100 is expected to open down 0.1%. U.K. retailers will be in focus after data showed retail sales for January were stronger than expected. Focus will also be on purchasing managers' data for France, Germany, the eurozone and the U.K. (jessica.fleetham@wsj.com)

Contact us in London. +44-20-7842-9288
Markettalk.eu@dowjones.com

data.title: U.K. Watchdog Fines Banks Over Information Sharing on Government Bonds
data.description:
By Elena Vardon

The U.K.'s competition regulator fined Citi, HSBC, Morgan Stanley and Royal Bank of Canada 104.5 million pounds ($132.3 million) after a probe into the exchange of sensitive information about government bonds.

The Competition and Markets Authority said Friday that it reached a settlement with the four banks in separate cases about the conduct of traders on specific instances between 2009 and 2013. It also settled with Deutsche Bank, which didn't receive a fine as it was granted immunity for reporting its conduct.

The CMA said its investigation found that individual traders shared competitively sensitive information about the pricing of U.K. government bonds--gilts and gilt asset swaps--on specific dates via one-to-one conversations on chatrooms.

"It is important that competitors decide their price and strategies independently in order to ensure effective competition in a market," the CMA said.

The banks have implemented compliance measures since the last exchanges to prevent this from happening again, the watchdog said.

The fines amount to 23.4 million pounds for HSBC, 29.7 million pounds for Morgan Stanley and 34.2 million pounds for RBC. Citi received a reduced fine of 17.2 million pounds as it applied for leniency during the investigation.

Neither Citi, HSBC, Morgan Stanley, Royal Bank of Canada nor Deutsche Bank immediately responded to requests for comment.

Write to Elena Vardon at elena.vardon@wsj.com

data.title: French Industry Sentiment Improves Despite Tariff Threat
data.description:
By Joshua Kirby

The mood at French factories brightened a little this month as borrowing costs come down, and despite the threat of tariffs on exports to the U.S.

An index of sentiment among French industry rose to 97 in February from 96 a month earlier, statistics agency Insee said Friday. That was a little better than economists had expected and suggests confidence is edging back toward its long-term average, represented by a reading of 100 on the index.

February's better mood was driven by manufacturers' growing confidence about their production over the coming months as order books rise, pointing to a recovery in demand for French industrial goods. That is despite the tariffs that U.S. President Trump has threatened to impose on European goods, including steel and aluminum and auto vehicles.

Write to Joshua Kirby at joshua.kirby@wsj.com; @joshualeokirby

data.title: European Stocks Seen Opening Slightly Higher, FTSE 100 Slightly Lower — Market Talk
data.description: European stocks are expected to open slightly higher, with Germany's DAX up 0.1% and France's CAC 40 up 0.3%, according to IG. Chinese stocks rose after Alibaba earnings buoyed sentiment toward Chinese tech stocks. The mood was less upbeat on U.S. stock markets, however, after weak guidance from retailer Walmart. The U.K.'s FTSE 100 is expected to open down 0.1%. U.K. retailers will be in focus after data showed retail sales for January were stronger than expected. Focus will also be on purchasing managers' data for France, Germany, the eurozone and the U.K. (jessica.fleetham@wsj.com)

  • Manufacturing confidence 97
  • Prior 95; revised to 96

data.title: EDF: Excellent Operational Performance in a Context of Lower Market Prices
EDF 2025 EBITDA Expected to Retreat Against Backdrop of Falling Market Prices
China Stocks Rally on Tech and AI Boost
data.description:
The Shanghai Composite rose 0.85% to close at 3,378, while the Shenzhen Component surged 1.82% to 10,991 on Friday, with mainland stocks reaching multi-month highs as Chinese technology and artificial intelligence-related stocks continued to attract strong market interest. The renewed optimism in the sector was primarily driven by DeepSeek’s promising AI model and its rapid adoption by major companies.

Adding to the bullish sentiment, Hong Kong-listed Alibaba jumped around 14% on strong earnings, fueled by its expanding AI initiatives.

Markets are also hopeful that Beijing will shift towards a more favorable regulatory environment for the private sector, following a years-long regulatory crackdown.

Notable gainers included Talkweb Information (+10%), Hygon Information (+15.3%), and Cambricon Technologies (+20%).

data.title: Shoucheng Holdings Unit to Establish Robot Company in Beijing
data.description:
Shoucheng Holdings' subsidiary Shouwo Investment Holdings plans to establish a new company focused on building a comprehensive robot industry ecosystem in Beijing, China, a Friday Hong Kong bourse filing said.

The new entity, Beijing Shoucheng Robot Technology Industry, will offer specialized industrial and financial services within the robot sector. Its focus will encompass several key areas, including sales agency, leasing, industry consultation, and supply chain management.

data.title: Alibaba Profit Surges 239% as Retail, AI-Driven Cloud Services Grow
data.description:
Alibaba Group Holding posted net income of 48.95 billion yuan for the December quarter, up 239% from a year earlier, according to a Thursday filing on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange.

Diluted earnings per share rose 261% to 2.55 yuan. Revenue climbed 8% to 280.15 billion yuan.

Alibaba's earnings got a boost from a rebound in its core online retail business and rapid expansion in artificial intelligence-driven cloud services.

Revenue from its Taobao and Tmall Group, which accounts for most of its earnings, rose 9%, while cloud computing revenue grew 13%. AI-related product revenue continued to see triple-digit growth for a sixth straight quarter.

Alibaba has been restructuring its business over the past year to focus on key areas. It has exited non-core assets, including the sale of its entire stake in retailer Sun Art for up to HK$12.3 billion and department store chain Intime for 7.4 billion yuan.

The company also repurchased $1.3 billion worth of shares during the quarter as part of its ongoing buyback program.

Alibaba's international e-commerce business posted strong growth, with revenue from its overseas arm rising 32%.

The company is investing in expansion in Europe and the Gulf region and has disclosed a joint venture with South Korea's Shinsegae to develop its AliExpress operations in the country.

Cloud Intelligence Group, which houses Alibaba's cloud services, saw revenue growth pick up after a slowdown earlier in the year.

data.title: StanChart's Pre-Tax Profit Slides in Q4; Expects Higher Income Ahead
data.description:
Standard Chartered reported a statutory pre-tax profit of $800 million in the fourth quarter of 2024, down 30% from $1.1 billion a year earlier.

Statutory earnings per share stood at $0.202 for the three months ended Dec. 31, 2024, versus $0.340 in the year-ago period, according to a Friday disclosure with the Hong Kong Exchange.

The figure is lower than the EPS estimate of $0.22 based on a consensus of analysts' estimates compiled by Visible Alpha.

Underlying pre-tax profit stood at $1 billion, while underlying EPS fell to $0.289 from $0.304.

The British lender's Q4 statutory net interest income fell 8% year over year to $1.71 billion. Meanwhile, underlying net interest income came in at $2.86 billion, up 20% year-on-year.

For the full year 2024, the bank recorded a statutory pre-tax profit of $6 billion, compared with $5.1 billion in 2023. The underlying net interest income was $10.4 billion.

The company declared a final dividend of $0.28 per share, to be paid on May 19.

The bank also announced a $1.5 billion share buyback as part of its plan to return at least $8 billion to shareholders cumulatively from 2024 to 2026.

Looking ahead, StanChart expects its operating income to increase 5% to 7% CAGR in 2023-2026 at constant currency rates.

data.title: Baazar Style Retail Opens New Store in West Bengal, India
data.description:
Baazar Style Retail has opened a new store at Domkal in West Bengal on Friday, according to a same-day disclosure to the Indian stock exchanges.

With this, the company's total number of stores has increased to 202.

Shares of the company were down over 2% in recent trade.

data.title: EDF 2024 EBIT EUR18.3B
Saint-Gobain Appoints Maud Thuaudet as New CFO
data.description:
(07:15 GMT) *Compagnie De Saint-Gobain Names Maud Thuaudet as CFO
(07:16 GMT) *Compagnie De Saint-Gobain: Thuaudet's Appointment Effective From April 1
(07:16 GMT) *Compagnie De Saint-Gobain: Thuaudet Joined Saint-Gobain in Sept 2019
(07:18 GMT) *Compagnie De Saint-Gobain: CFO Sreedhar N. Will Become Asia-Pacific Region CEO
(07:32 GMT) Saint-Gobain Appoints Maud Thuaudet as New CFO

By Nina Kienle

Compagnie De Saint-Gobain said it appointed Maud Thuaudet as new chief financial officer, effective April 1.

The French building materials company on Friday said Thuaudet will succeed Sreedhar N., who held the position for more than six years.

The company appointed Sreedhar N. chief executive officer for the Asia-Pacific region, including Australia and New Zealand and India, effective also April 1, it said.

Write to Nina Kienle at nina.kienle@wsj.com

data.title: Totally CEO to Step Down
data.description:
By Michael Susin

Totally said Chief Executive Wendy Lawrence will step down from the company with immediate effect without disclosing further details.

The provider of healthcare services in the U.K. and Ireland on Friday said it intends to start a formal recruitment process as soon as possible to replace Lawrence, who was 12 years in the role.

In the meantime, Totally's Chief Operating Officer Prasad Godbole has been appointed as interim CEO with immediate effect.

Write to Michael Susin at michael.susin@wsj.com

data.title: Petronas Chemicals Shares Jump After Sharply Higher Quarterly Profit
data.description:
By Ying Xian Wong

Petronas Chemicals shares rose sharply after the Malaysian petrochemical company reported a more than fourfold increase in fourth-quarter profit.

The company's shares jumped as much as 11% in Friday afternoon trade, and were last 8.9% higher at 4.03 ringgit, trimming year-to-date losses to about 22%.

Petronas Chemicals said earlier Friday that fourth-quarter net profit jumped to 519 million ringgit, equivalent to $117.2 million, from 112 million ringgit for the same period a year ago. The profit increase was mainly due to higher sales volumes and foreign-exchange gains on the revaluation of payables for a joint operation entity.

Quarterly revenue was 3.4% higher at 7.46 billion ringgit.

Looking ahead, market conditions are expected to remain largely unchanged in 2025 amid uncertainties stemming from shifting economic policies around the world, potential retaliatory actions by affected countries and ongoing geopolitical events, Petronas Chemicals Chief Executive Mazuin Ismail said in a statement.

Meanwhile, the oversupply in global petrochemicals remains a challenge, with capacity additions expected to outpace demand growth by about 50% in 2025, he said.

Since late 2024, prices and spreads in olefins and derivatives have declined and narrowed, with signs that Southeast Asian integrated spreads may stay weak, he added.

Write to Ying Xian Wong at yingxian.wong@wsj.com
